title: RealMLP/PyTabKit two-seed n_ens=40 5-fold OOF validation completed with alternate-CV RealMLP, CatBoost, nested OOF-se...
summary: RealMLP/PyTabKit two-seed n_ens=40 5-fold OOF validation completed with alternate-CV RealMLP, CatBoost, nested OOF-selected raw/logit/rankit blending, and nested fold-bagged test blend configurations.
rationale: I will keep the trained RealMLP core, alternate-CV RealMLP stream, CatBoost expert, feature engineering, folds, target encoding, and model hyperparameters unchanged. The single improvement is to make the final test-time blend mirror the nested validation procedure by bagging the blend configurations selected inside each meta fold. This avoids depending on one full-OOF-selected blend for the submission, which was the main remaining overfit risk in the previous solution. The printed primary metric remains the nested meta-OOF ROC AUC, while diagnostics still report the full-OOF-selected blend for comparison.
implementation_hint: I will keep the trained RealMLP core, alternate-CV RealMLP stream, CatBoost expert, feature engineering, folds, target encoding, and model hyperparameters unchanged. The single improvement is to make the final test-time blend mirror the nested validation procedure by bagging the blend configurations selected inside each meta fold. This avoids depending on one full-OOF-selected blend for the submission, which was the main remaining overfit risk in the previous solution. The printed primary metric remains the nested meta-OOF ROC AUC, while diagnostics still report the full-OOF-selected blend for comparison.
expected_effect: RealMLP/PyTabKit two-seed n_ens=40 5-fold OOF validation completed with alternate-CV RealMLP, CatBoost, nested OOF-selected raw/logit/rankit blending, and nested fold-bagged test blend configurations.
risk: The reported primary AUC uses nested meta-OOF blend selection; submission predictions average the nested fold-selected blend configurations instead of relying on a single full-OOF-selected test blend.
